title: The Mystery of the Snake Store Bups
summary: Hattie the mouse is walking through town when she visits the curious Snake Store, which sells a unique type of lollipop known as 'bups.' While choosing a flavor, Hattie runs into her friend Mischief and Mischief's mother, who works as the store's cashier. Hattie chooses a chocolate bup while Mischief picks vanilla. The two friends head to the local park to enjoy the swing set and play together. Their fun is interrupted by a sudden rainstorm, prompting the pair to hurry back to Hattie's nearby house for shelter.

highlightQuote.text: Bups are a kind of lollipop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That'll just be sleeping here on this chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You will? Okay. Do you want to do Stars and Stories?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, put my blanket on me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I will.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Ready for your blanket? Here's your blanket. Okay, and I'll turn out the lamp. And now it's Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh, it's about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Tell me the story about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I cannot.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You can't? Why not?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Because I don't know what's going to be. Tell story and then and I'll know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. I'm just thinking about what to tell a story about.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Hattie was walking through town. And she passed a very interesting kind of store. What kind of store was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a snake store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A what store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A snake store. Snake store.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did they sell in that store?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some bups.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some what?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Some bups.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some bups? What are bups?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Bups are a kind of lollipop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, bups are a kind of lollipop. Hattie thought to herself, 'Huh...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, put my blanket on me.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay, here, I'll fix it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hattie went into the store and she was looking at the bups, which are a kind of lollipop. And she was trying to decide whether she should try the watermelon flavored one or the lime flavored one, when someone said, 'Oh, hello Hattie!' It was Hattie's friend. Who was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Um, maybe it was...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! I don't know. You don't know either!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I don't know either?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I think you don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Tell the story and maybe you'll find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's a good idea. I'll just keep telling the story and I'll find out who it was. And Hattie said, 'Oh, hello, you look familiar, but I don't remember who you are.' And the other creature, what kind of creature was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the other creature... what kind of creature was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The other creature was a dog.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No! Not a dog!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what kind of creature was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A cat?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: An owl?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Just keep telling tell the story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Of course. It was Shimmer Fish.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, fish. No, you have to keep doing story and you find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie bumped into a friend in the store that sold the bups, but we'll never know who it was because after that Hattie decided which flavor to buy of the lollipop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Oh maybe it was... it was Mischief and her mom. I remember.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was Mischief and her mom. They were shopping in the store too. And what...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No. Mischief's mom was the cashier.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mischief's mom was the cashier, of course. And so what flavor of lollipop bup did Hattie Mouse buy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Chocolate.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A chocolate one, that sounds delicious. And Mischief decided to get one too. What flavor did she get?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Vanilla.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A vanilla. And then Mischief's mom said, 'Okay everyone, boop-boop, there's your bups. Enjoy them.' And Mischief said, 'Hey Hattie, would you like to come out with me? I'm going to go to the park.' And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know what Mischief say.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Mischief said, 'You know they have a swing set there. Do you want to come with me?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes? Okay, let's go! And so together, Mischief Mouse and Hattie the Mouse went outside and they went carefully across the street. They looked both ways first and then they crossed the street and then they got to the park. And there was a little playground and in the playground there was a swing set. And there were two swings and they were both free. And Mischief said, 'I'm going to jump on this swing. Do you want to go on the other one, Hattie Mouse?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they both got on their swings and they swung back and forth. Swing, swing, swing, swing.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hang up now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you getting up now? Okay.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then Mischief Mouse said, 'I'm a little tired of swinging. I'm going to hop right off.' And she went 'hop' and got off the swing. And then Hattie the Mouse, what did she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She was just playing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: She was just playing. And so they ran around the playground playing. Um, and then what happened next?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling story and then find out.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. I'll keep telling the story and then we'll find out. Hmm... and then, uh-oh, they heard a little... a little dribble-drop sound. A little splash. A little drop that went 'plop' right on top of Hattie the Mouse's head. And she looked up at the sky and what did she say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know what's that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, she saw that the sky had turned gray and there were big gray clouds.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And it was raining and Mischief go back to your house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mischief said, 'Let's go back to your house!' and Hattie said, 'Yes, come on to my house, it's right nearby!'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they ran, ran, ran through the park, because Hattie the Mouse's house was just on the other side of the park. And Hattie opened the door, and Hattie and Mischief went inside. And what did they do there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were just going... Now are we done for Stars and Stories? I think I'm done.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Well... Stars and Stories time is almost over but we still have a little bit more time. We could tell one more part of the story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm done. I think I'm done because I have because I need to go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. Well then.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Mischief
characters.description: Proprietor of the analog-scented tape store on the corner of Poplar and Popcornioni, this avian known as Velociraptorning is Hattie’s loyal companion and the devoted mother of Mistress Mouse. Whether aiding Oscar on his adventures from their shared burrow or tending her wall of magnetic spools, she remains a soul of simple pleasures—sketching in sun-drenched parks, sharing meals of corn, and admiring her daughter’s new hat with a prideful, artistic eye.

characters.name: Mischief's Mom
characters.description: The friendly cashier at the local Snake Store.

places.name: Snake Store
places.description: A curious shop that sells special lollipops called 'bups'.

places.name: The Park
places.description: A local recreation area with a swing set and a playground.

places.name: Hattie's House
places.description: Nestled in a sun-drenched tree across from the town park, Hattie’s arboreal sanctuary on Mouse Street doubles as a vibrant Cornell Street workshop for designing hats and a peaceful retreat for resting after long days of painting. This cozy home, cluttered with the tools of her trade, is a place of shared morning breakfasts with Mischief and savory meals cooked alongside her Nana. Whether lulled by the rhythmic drumming of rainfall or the quiet exhaustion of her artistry, Hattie finds her solace here, drifting into restorative naps amidst the creative clutter.

places.name: Town
places.description: The urban area where Hattie takes her daily strolls.
